cards.title: Discovery Tetrad
cards.direction: A process-level audit over reasoning itself: separate the goal from the witness offered for it, score each by four gates, and read out a 25-cell outcome vocabulary for completed discovery traces.

witnesses.headline: Five heterogeneous substrates (FLT, SLT, FirstProof, AI-Scientist v1 & v2) audited under one frozen instrument — judge + schema + validator — with zero schema failures.
witnesses.note: Goal vs witness, each scored by the four gates → a 16-cell goal map and a 9-cell witness cascade. This atlas is laid out in that grammar; the audit code and dataset are folded here as one card.

cards.title: PAC Learnability · Characterizations
cards.direction: PAC learnability, finite VC dimension, finite sample compression with side information, and the Rademacher and growth-function conditions are equivalent, and the five-way equivalence is formally verified in Lean 4. A constructive separation shows the null-measurable-target condition for PAC learning is strictly weaker than the Krapp-Wirth Borel condition.
cards.note: The separation theorem takes a non-Borel analytic set as an explicit hypothesis and derives the separation from it.

witnesses.claim: Five-way equivalence: PAC learnability, finite VC dimension, finite compression, Rademacher, and growth conditions coincide.
witnesses.code:
theorem fundamental_theorem (X : Type u) [MeasurableSpace X]
    [MeasurableSingletonClass X]
    (C : ConceptClass X Bool)
    [MeasurableConceptClass X C] :
    (PACLearnable X C ↔ VCDim X C < ⊤) ∧
    ((VCDim X C < ⊤) ↔ ∃ (k : ℕ) (cs : CompressionSchemeWithInfo0 X Bool C), cs.size = k) ∧ ...

witnesses.claim: A concept class is PAC learnable iff its VC dimension is finite.
witnesses.code:
theorem vc_characterization (X : Type u) [MeasurableSpace X]
    [MeasurableSingletonClass X]
    (C : ConceptClass X Bool)
    [MeasurableConceptClass X C] :
    PACLearnable X C ↔ VCDim X C < ⊤ :=

witnesses.claim: Moran-Yehudayoff equivalence: finite VC dimension iff a finite sample compression scheme with side information exists.
witnesses.code:
theorem fundamental_vc_compression_with_info
    (X : Type u) (C : ConceptClass X Bool) :
    (VCDim X C < ⊤) ↔
    (∃ (k : ℕ) (cs : CompressionSchemeWithInfo0 X Bool C), cs.size = k) :=

witnesses.claim: Given a non-Borel analytic set, the null-measurable-target condition is separated from the Borel-target condition for PAC learning.
witnesses.code:
theorem analytic_nonborel_set_gives_measTarget_separation
    (A : Set ℝ)
    (hA_an : MeasureTheory.AnalyticSet A)
    (hA_non : ¬ MeasurableSet A) :
    KrappWirthSeparationMeasTarget :=

cards.title: Online and Limit Learning · Mistake Bounds
cards.direction: Online learnability of a concept class is equivalent to finiteness of its Littlestone dimension, and for nonempty classes the optimal mistake bound equals the Littlestone dimension exactly. In the identification-in-the-limit setting, EX-learnability is characterized by a finite mind-change count, and Gold's theorem rules out EX-identification of any class containing all finite sets together with an infinite set; the proof runs through a fully explicit adversarial locking sequence.

witnesses.claim: A concept class is online learnable iff its Littlestone dimension is finite.
witnesses.code:
theorem littlestone_characterization (X : Type) (C : ConceptClass X Bool) :
    OnlineLearnable X Bool C ↔ LittlestoneDim X C < ⊤ :=

witnesses.claim: The optimal mistake bound equals the Littlestone dimension for nonempty concept classes.
witnesses.code:
theorem optimal_mistake_bound_eq_ldim (X : Type)
    (C : ConceptClass X Bool) (hne : C.Nonempty) :
    (↑(OptimalMistakeBound X C) : WithBot (WithTop ℕ)) = LittlestoneDim X C :=

witnesses.claim: No learner can EX-identify a class containing all finite sets plus an infinite set.
witnesses.code:
theorem gold_theorem (X : Type u) [Countable X] [DecidableEq X]
    (C : ConceptClass X Bool)
    (h_finite : ∀ (S : Finset X), (fun x => decide (x ∈ S)) ∈ C)
    (h_infinite : ∃ c ∈ C, Set.Infinite { x | c x = true }) :
    ¬ EXLearnable X C :=

witnesses.claim: A class is EX-learnable iff some learner makes only finitely many mind changes on every text presentation.
witnesses.code:
theorem mind_change_characterization (X : Type u)
    (C : ConceptClass X Bool) :
    EXLearnable X C ↔
      ∃ (L : GoldLearner X Bool),
        ∀ (c : Concept X Bool), c ∈ C →
          ∀ (T : TextPresentation X c),
            MindChangeOrdinal X L c T.toDataStream < Ordinal.omega0 :=

cards.title: Concentration and Generalization
cards.direction: The McAllester PAC-Bayes bound is proved from first principles via Hoeffding's inequality and Jensen's inequality: for a finite hypothesis class, with probability at least 1 - delta over m independent samples, the Gibbs error of every posterior Q is at most its empirical Gibbs error plus sqrt((H_cross(Q, P) + log(1/delta)) / (2m)). The Efron-Stein inequality bounds the variance of a function of n independent random variables in a product probability space by the sum of coordinate-wise conditional variance terms.
cards.note: The PAC-Bayes statement is proved for a finite hypothesis class with an everywhere-positive prior.

witnesses.claim: McAllester bound for a finite hypothesis class: Gibbs error at most empirical Gibbs error plus sqrt((H_cross(Q, P) + log(1/delta)) / (2m)).
witnesses.code:
theorem pac_bayes_finite {X : Type u} [MeasurableSpace X]
    {H : Type*} [Fintype H] [Nonempty H]
    (D : MeasureTheory.Measure X) [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure D]
    (c : Concept X Bool) (hc_meas : Measurable c)
    (hs : H → Concept X Bool) (hhs_meas : ∀ h, Measurable (hs h))
    (P : FinitePMF H) (hP_pos : ∀ h, 0 < P.prob h)
    (m : ℕ) (hm : 0 < m) (δ : ℝ) (hδ : 0 < δ) (hδ1 : δ ≤ 1) :
    MeasureTheory.Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin m => D)
      { S : Fin m → X |
        ∀ (Q : FinitePMF H),
          gibbsError Q hs c D ≤
            gibbsEmpError Q hs c S +
            Real.sqrt ((crossEntropyFinitePMF Q P + Real.log (1 / δ)) / (2 * ↑m)) }
      ≥ ENNReal.ofReal (1 - δ) :=

witnesses.claim: Efron-Stein: the variance of a function of n independent variables is bounded by summed conditional variance terms.
witnesses.code:
theorem efronStein (f : (Fin n → Ω) → ℝ) (hf : MemLp f 2 μˢ) :
    variance f μˢ ≤ ∑ i : Fin n, ∫ x, (f x - condExpExceptCoord (μs := μs) i f x)^2 ∂μˢ :=
